# ctrlplane_secret (Resource)

Manages a secret in Ctrlplane. A secret is a reference to a value stored in a secret provider; the value itself is never held in Terraform state.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ctrlplane_secret_provider" "aws" {
  name = "aws-prod"
  type = "aws-secrets-manager"
  config_wo = jsonencode({
    region = "us-east-1"
  })
  config_wo_version = 1
}

resource "ctrlplane_secret" "db_password" {
  scope       = "workspace"
  name        = "db-password"
  provider_id = ctrlplane_secret_provider.aws.id
  path        = ["secret", "data", "db"]
  key         = "password"
  version     = "v1"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) The key addressing the secret value within the provider
- `name` (String) The name of the secret
- `path` (List of String) The path segments addressing the secret within the provider
- `provider_id` (String) The ID of the secret provider that holds the secret value
- `scope` (String) The scope of the secret (e.g. workspace, deployment, environment, resource, job_agent). Changing this forces a new secret.

### Optional

- `deployment_id` (String) The deployment this secret is scoped to. Changing this forces a new secret.
- `environment_id` (String) The environment this secret is scoped to. Changing this forces a new secret.
- `job_agent_id` (String) The job agent this secret is scoped to. Changing this forces a new secret.
- `resource_id` (String) The resource this secret is scoped to. Changing this forces a new secret.
- `version` (String) The version of the secret value to reference

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the secret
